entry.title: Re: SAP versions and modules
entry.content:
Hey @Inka 

You're welcome! 😊 Great questions! So, regarding FSCM vs. SAP S/4HANA Collections Management and Dispute Management, you're right that the concept is similar, but there could be various reasons why some still prefer FSCM. It could be familiarity, existing setups, or specific functionalities.

As for SAP GUI vs. Fiori Launchpad, it's interesting to note that many customers still use SAP GUI. It's not necessarily about cost; sometimes, it's about the ease of transition or the specific needs of the business.

Hope this helps! Let me know if you have any more questions! 😊
Warm regards,
H

entry.title: Re: SAP Transportation Management Container Numbers ISO 6346
entry.content:
Thanks Regerio for the blog. 

I have a question. What is the source database to validate the container no?

entry.title: Provision SAC
entry.content:
Hello

Appreciate Your Advise 

I need to calculate the yearly provision by year for contract 

For example

contract 1000 :

The Amount at the begging is 420M for year 2023

Starting year 2027 i need to have provision for 3 Years

Thank mean in 2027 i will make a financial provision of 140M and that so until 2029

 

How is the best way to create this calculation?

i need to do data action that contain if

if the year at the property of the contract is => then write the amount of the contract divided by contract duration period on the measure "Changes" 

and change the closing amount of this year and transfer it to the beginning amount  for the next year

Appreciate your help to guide me which script i need to do

 

i have created the properties for the contract dimension 

my year granularity is set to year

i am trying to create an action button for creating this calculation

i have  4 measures : start amount, Begging period, Changes and End period

How can i write in the action button that it will check the year?
and write down the amount at the "Changes" Measures?
and after it to take the balance and copy it to the next year begging

I have tried this script but i have error

MEMBERSET [d/Date].[p/YEAR] = RESULTLOOKUP([d/LegalDoc].[p/DateStart]) to next (RESULTLOOKUP([d/LegalDoc].[p/TermDuration]),"Year",RESULTLOOKUP([d/LegalDoc].[p/DateStart]))
 
FOREACH [d/Date]
DATA([d/Measures]="Changes",[d/IAA_GLACCOUNT]="522383200") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="StartAmount") / RESULTLOOKUP([d/LegalDoc].[p/TermDuration])
 
ENDFOR

Thank you very much


@N1kh1l 
@SAPSupport

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 2
entry.content:
well, I used an SDK in the first place, so here you go:

// a JSON object was enough, but the idea is learning...
import { CloudEvent, CloudEventV1 } from "cloudevents"
import { v1 } from "uuid"
import Ajv from "ajv"
import schema from "./cloudeventschema.json"

const payload: CloudEventV1<any> = {
  id: v1(),
  specversion: "1.0",
  source: "week1testprog",
  type: "week1testprog.demoevent",
  datacontenttype: "application/json",
  data: {
    salutation: "hello"
  }
}

const msg = new CloudEvent(payload) // basically checks the schema and adds the time

// redundant - check schema
const validator = new Ajv({ strict: false }).compile(schema)
const valid = validator(msg)
if (valid) console.log(JSON.stringify(msg, null, 1))
else throw new Error("Invalid cloud event")

The output includes a bunch of warning from a JSON schema validator. Should be totally redundant but wanted to try it

Priyanka

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 2
entry.content:
Sending Challenge 2

from cloudevents.http import CloudEvent
from cloudevents.conversion import to_binary
import requests

username = "David"
userLastName = "Gonzalez"
password = "MTIzNA=="
country = "CL"
subject = "Creacion Usuarios" + username

# Create a CloudEvent
attributes = {
    "type": "com.sap.build.app.Launched.v1",
    "source": "https://cloud.dagoca.com/events/spec/pull",
    "subject": subject,
}
data = { 
   "users":[
      {
          "name": username,
          "lastName": userLastName,
          "password": password,
          "country": country

      }  
   ]
  }
event = CloudEvent(attributes, data)

# Creates the HTTP request representation of the CloudEvent in binary content mode
headers, body = to_binary(event)

print(body)

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Problem with SAP BW Query performance for Exception aggregation KPI
entry.content:
Hi,

there's a performance problem with a exception aggregation keyfigure “#items”

The system doesn’t make a "hana push down" to calculate the exception aggregation keyfigure "#items"

"# items" keyfigure definition =>

 

Result in SAP BW =>

  • Questions:
    • Is there no Push down bcs of the underlined keyfigure “YLBKUMH” (bcs of non cumulative keyfigure or the exception aggregation for this keyfigure)?
      • If yes, what we can do?
    • Is there maybe another problem, that there’s no push down to hana?

Thx for feedback

br Oliver

entry.title: Re: SPAU TR not given to SUM tool
entry.content: You said that you didn't "provide TR" in DEV for SPAU/SPDD (not sure to understand what you DID then) but you moved TR (?) to Quality, I don't understand, what TR did you move? What "a program" did you execute? I think it's a serious problem if you didn't follow the procedure, so restore DEV and redo the procedure. It's up to you if you want to risk issues to continue like that. If you like risk, then do SPAU on QAS and continue (I'm not able to evaluate the risk level).

Thanks,

Shashi

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP BW Request Housekeeping
entry.content:
Hi BW enthusiasts!
In this blog post, I would like to dive deeper into Housekeeping, an area sometimes a bit neglected in BW-systems. The focus here is actually on BW Request Housekeeping.

To start with, it is important to understand which kind of request management concepts are available and which one applies for your specific SAP BW environment:

1) The classic one: Request Status Management (RSSM) is the original concept from SAP NetWeaver BW and there in use for a long time already for all classic InfoProviders CUBE, ODSO, IOBJ and DEST.
The RequestID is based on a prefix and a following 25 characters long randomly generated unique identifier. Typical prefixes are REQU_* for InfoPackage requests, DTPR_* for DTP requests, and ODSR_* for ODSO activation requests.

2) The new one: Request Status and Process Management (RSPM) is the revised concept for ADSOs in SAP NetWeaver BW 7.50 on HANA. In SAP BW/4HANA RSPM is used for all InfoProviders (ADSO, IOBJ, DEST) and RSSM is decommissioned.
The Request ID is now called Request TSN (Transaction Sequence No), which is a timestamp and is derived from the ODQ Transaction ID.

As a first summary the focus of your individual BW request housekeeping concept is either on RSSM processes/tools or RSPM processes/tools. OR, in case you run BW on HANA, then often both approaches apply as classic objects and ADSOs often coexist side-by-side.

WHY bothering with BW request housekeeping?
In general, request meta information (all details your find in a request monitor like timestamps, runtime, package sizes, record amount, warnings etc) is stored in BW backend tables. These tables grow continuously based on the daily data processing activities. If housekeeping is not done, it will have a negative impact on the…

  • overall system size,
  • data management performance (loading, activation, delta determination etc.),
  • waiting time for InfoProvider administration in Tr. RSA1/RSMNG, or BW/4HANA Cockpit, or
  • preparation of a move to BW/4HANA or BW Bridge. Leveraging the SAP transfer tools (BW/4HANA inplace or remote approach / BW Bridge remote approach) requires the number or requests in classic InfoProviders to be reasonable (<1.000), see also warnings in your Readiness Check.

WHAT is done during BW request housekeeping?
Request Housekeeping deletes the request details (meta data) from the core runtime BW backend tables. This means, details of the request history will be removed which is older than a customer specific number of days. However, no need to be afraid: The real BW data is not deleted - master data and transactional data residing in the InfoProviders is NOT affected! Housekeeping only shrinks the request history behind them!
Also, good to know: During the housekeeping process, the objects are not locked, and they are available for loading/reporting without restrictions. Still, it is clear it is better to run the cleanup not during business hours.

As you see in the figure below, the housekeeping process removes the detail data from the original tables and moves it to housekeeping tables in a compressed form. If that is not enough (normally however this is not the case), you can even move all to an external archive (RSSM details here, for RSPM see SAP notes 2867347 and 2980697).

HOW to analyze the status quo?
RSSM: Tr. RSREQREDUCE provides the full list of InfoProviders with critical no of requests.
The same information is provided by table RSMDATASTATE_EXT.

RSPM: In SAP BW/4HANA, leverage the Cockpit apps for Request Management in ADSOs, InfoObjects or Open Hub Destinations and sort the column of 'Successful Requests' properly.

In SAP BW on HANA it is a little more challenging as these apps are missing. You can either use the new CDS-based technical content (aka "statistical analysis"). In Tr. RSRT following sources are available which both provide a key figure "No of successful requests" per ADSO:
2CRVCREQUEST/!2CRVCREQUEST or 2CRVCREQUEST/2CRVCREQQRY

The second BW on HANA option is to run following SQL statement (e.g. in Tr. DB02-SQL Editor) to get the ADSOs in most requests in descending order:
SELECT DATATARGET, COUNT(*) NUM_OF_REQUESTS
FROM RSPMREQUEST
GROUP BY DATATARGET
ORDER BY NUM_OF_REQUESTS DESC

HOW to run BW request housekeeping?
RSSM Request Housekeeping can be triggered and scheduled from Tr. RSREQREDUCE based on the two reports RSSM_REDUCE_REQUESTLIST and RSSM_AUTODEL_REQU_MASTER_TEXT. Thorough documentation here.
For RSPM, SAP provides report RSPM_HOUSEKEEPING which can be scheduled via Process Chains (in BW 7.5 as ABAP variant, in BW/4HANA there is a new dedicated variant). Thorough documentation here.

WHICH Requests can be removed?
You can clean up a request if it is not available as physical entity. This means, it is not stored in an InfoProvider table anymore (e.g., in an ODSO/ADSO inbound queue to change log table).

  1. To start with, all deleted requests can be removed from all InfoProviders.
  2. In general, all requests can be removed from following InfoProviders: ODSO/ADSO (type direct update), IOBJ and DEST
  3. Requests can also be removed if they meet following prerequisites:
    • ODSO/ADSO (type standard), CUBE/ADSO (type data mart)
      DTP loading requests can be deleted from the inbound queue if they are activated / compressed already.
    • ODSO/ADSO (type standard):
      Change log requests can be removed if they have been deleted from Change log. This is only possible if the delta has been provisioned to all connected data targets.

Finally, HOW to set the whole process up properly?

  1. Define your individual housekeeping strategy. This means you have to make a decision how many days of request history should be kept in your BW landscape.
  2. For RSPM: Depending on your SP-level, check whether some recommended SAP corrections need to be installed (see SAP note 3137171).
  3. Test housekeeping on a few objects (e.g. 1 ODSO/ADSO, 1 IOBJ, 1 DEST).
  4. Run housekeeping once for the whole DEV-system (e.g. with > 1000 days).
  5. For RSPM: Transport SAP notes (2) to the next system.
  6. Run housekeeping in tiers until you reach the defined limit of of days (1), depending on the age of the system, for example 1st run > 1500 days, 2nd one > 1000 days, then >800 days… In a large system each run could take a few hours.
  7. When the defined limit (1) is reached: Schedule housekeeping, e.g. for RSPM in a weekly process chain like the following one:


Useful sources:

  • SAP note 3137171 - Housekeeping for Request Administration tables (RSPM* tables)
  • RSSM Housekeeping
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/bwdabc/3361383396.html
  • RSPM Housekeeping
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/bwdabc/3564975514.html
  • SAP Help Portal (BW/4HANA)
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BW4HANA/b3701cd3826440618ef938d74dc93c51/b6474ca70984453b89501a7291698f30.html?version=2.0.14

entry.title: IBP Time Series Supply-Component/Resource changes in Production Order
entry.content:
Typically when we bring in Production order from ECC/S4- we only bring in the PV/qty/date and use the IBP prodn src header to do the BOM explosion /Resource within IBP.

However if there are components changed or routing operation-resource changes in ECC/S4 Production Order
- how can we bring this into IBP timeseries

I know there is a functionality in order series now

Just checking to see if anyone has done any creative solutioning around this.

Thanks

Phalgun Ayyagari

entry.title: Error in Condition Contract Settlement Pricing DIFF Condition types for Rounding off the Gross Value
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
We have requirement to round off the Gross Value (Net Value + Tax) of pricing for that We are using standard condition type DIFF (Round Off) with Requirement Routine 13, Alt Condition type 16 and Alt Condition Value 4 in settlement pricing procedure.
When we are creating settlement document system is giving error: A Balance of INR 0.20- exists in document 0000001. Error: WS(Settlement Management) Message no. 123
We are unable to resolve the error since we are using standard condition type and routine.
Request your help in resolving the error which will be highly appreciable.
Regards
Prashant Ray


Error Replication:
User getting the Gross amount INR 120, 000.01 which we want round off to INR 120,000.00

Applied standard Condition type DIFF with standard pricing routines 13, 16 & 4

While creating Settlement getting error.

entry.title: SAP Gateway Project Extn/Redefinition - Issue with call to extension
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a common requirement to extend standard SAP gateway project and redefine couple of DPC extension class methods to add some additional logic to the standard one. We could do that, but when we are calling the standard UI5 application, it does not make call extension project DPC classes though we are using the standard OData Service with/without redefinition.

System version: SAP S/4HANA 2022 FPS 02.

To achieve the same, we have extended a standard Gateway Project /SCWM/PACK_OUTBDLV using a Redefine Service option into a custom one ZSCWM_PACK_OUTBDLV. We have generated the classes(DPC, DPC ext, MPC, MPC ext, Model and service) and did not  create any new OData service as we would like to use the standard OData service.

 

In the custom DPC extn class, we have redefined the method: VALIDATEACTIONSE_CREATE_ENTITY in to perform some pre and post calculations. 

Now, when I am calling the standard Ui5 project, it does not go into the extended gateway project DPC extension class, it is just going to standard class.

Its a simple requirement, but it does not work as expected. Was searching in SAP help and blogs, there is no information available to solve the issue.

What could be issue here, what am I missed here?

Thank you in advance.

 

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Error In condition Contract Settlement (Message WS123)
entry.content: Hi, Did you find out solution?, we are facing same issue

entry.title: How sending data through RFC using register sever program ID work?and handler was not installed erro
entry.content:
Hi, I'm currently working on sending and receiving data through RFC between SAP to Non-SAP,
and having difficulty understanding how Registered Server Program work. 

Here are 2 RFC s they require. .

A .Sap Receive data (data is sent from non-SAP to SAP. data is saved in SAP after receiving data) 

B .Sap Send data&receive result (data is sent from SAP to non-SAP and  non-SAP will send back result Sucess/Fail to SAP) 

 

A RFC is simply done by RFC being called on non-sap part.

For B RFC, I thought it'd be done same that they will call by using connector, But they require SAP Program to directly send(push) data so taht they will return the result back to SAP.

I asked them how theyll fetch data, and their reqmt was SAP to Create RFC desitnation and Registered Server Program. 

So now,  RFC Destiantion 'ZTEST' is type TCP/IP created which contains  Program ID is 'ZTEST'   as registered Server Program n SM59, and the connection test is success . 

In the program for sending data.. 

call function 'ZTEST_RFC_EXPORT' 

DESTINATION ZTEST

TABLES . .. 

 

but it's returning short dump for "handler for ZTEST_RFC_EXPORT was not installed

 

 I need help how sending data through RFC work just by registerng registered server program work.. whether it is correct step to send & get return data from non-sap system , and  if it is why this dump is returning. 

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: PIS e COFINS em outras Bases

entry.title: Re: I would like to ask for assistance with regarding the suggested solutions for the following four

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 2
entry.content:
@MarcelloUrbaniI've seen those format errors before.... You can include the formats by adding them to ajv. You will also need to npm install ajv-formats. See ajv-formats.

 

const Ajv = require('ajv');
const addFormats = require("ajv-formats");

const ajv = new Ajv();
addFormats(ajv);

entry.title: Re: French Character transfer issue in the file to Partner non-SAP-systems
entry.content:
While it has been at least a decade since I had hands-on involvement in Unicode system conversions, and my area was in the data transfer optimization, I recall we had to convert systems in phases as the business world could not stop entirely for any appreciable time. I remember that after R/3 systems were converted much testing was needed as legacy non-Unicode SAP code bases have a variety of character encodings primarily based on their age and crustiness. The statement:

OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE message ws_msg ENCODING DEFAULT

I think you need to know the encoding on both sides, and assume one side is definitely Unicode.

See, perhaps:

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_752_index_htm/7.52/en-US/abapopen_dataset_encoding.htm

"... Writes German umlaut characters to a non-Unicode code page. This code page is then extracted from the database table TCP0C and used to open the file as a a legacy text file."

DATA(dset) = 'test.dat'.
OPEN DATASET dset FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING NON-UNICODE.

"Code page maintenance is more guidelines than actual rules."

entry.title: Audio stream in AppGyver Community Edition
entry.content: I have built an app that plays several audio files from external URLs, using a scroll view linked in repeat to a page variable. Each element of the scroll view is linked to an audio streaming URL, and I can listen to the music but have been unable to display the titles and performers that are displayed in the respective remote players, changing with each track. Can you please help me with this? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA OP/Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, in general we have got the solution up and running in the S/4HANA On-Premise system. One observation is that the ship-to BP comes from the customer number assignment for the receiving plant when we assigned a storage location which derived a different customer BP on the Shipping tab of the STO. The outbound delivery actually takes the customer BP on the Shipping tab of the STO. This is inconsistent and would cause confusion for the users. Is this standard or considered a bug? I have looked up OSS but could not find any relevant notes. Appreciate any clarification on the above. Cheers.

Siow Chen

entry.title: BPMon monitoring for Number range
entry.content:
• How to put the correct threshold value for yellow and red for the key figure 'Number range intervals with critical fill level'?
  • How the filling level can be calculated? 

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BPMon monitoring for Number range
entry.content:
The return value of the Key Figure 'Number range intervals with critical fill level' will be the count of the number range which exceeds the threshold of Filling level if you configured it.
Therefore, the threshold in the parameter should be the number of 'Number range objects' which exceeds the threshold.

Let's say followings are configured in the parameter set.
- Threshold for Yellow: 0
- Threshold for Red: 1

And the filling level is set higher than 90%.

If there is only one Number range object which filling level is over 90%, the key figure will return the count 1. As this return value exceeds the value of 'Threshold for Yellow' but didn't exceeds so the value of 'Threshold for Red', an alert will occur with the yellow rating.

If there are 2 Number range objects which filling level is over 90%, the key figure will return the count 2. As this return value exceeds the value of 'Threshold for Red', an alert will occur with the red rating.

If you felt difficulties when checking the current filling level of the number range objects, I recommend you to use the report 'TBI_REPORTS' via the transaction ST13 as mentioned in the KBA 2363076.
Via this report, you can simulate the key figure to check what kind of values will be returned by the key figure. And when you displayed the detail list from the result, you can also check the corresponding entries of Number ranges with its currently filling level %.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

entry.title: Re: No response when clicking close button on fragement i...
entry.content: Update the fragement with the following :

entry.title: Re: No response when clicking close button on fragement i...
entry.content:
Update the fragement with the following: 

<core:FragmentDefinition  xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:l="sap.ui.layout" xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:u="sap.ui.unified" xmlns:upload="sap.m.upload" >
	<Dialog id="uploadDialogSet" title="File Upload" core:require="{handler: 'freightorderattachmgt/ext/fileHandle/FileHandle'}">
        <content>
            <upload:UploadSet uploadEnabled="true" id="uploadSet" items="{path: '/', templateShareable: false}" fileTypes="png,pdf" maxFileNameLength="200" beforeUploadStarts="handler.onBeforeUploadStart" uploadCompleted="handler.onUploadSetComplete" afterItemRemoved="handler.onItemRemoved"
            terminationEnabled="true">
                <upload:UploadSetItem id="_IDGenUploadSetItem1" visibleRemove="true" visibleEdit="false" fileName="{name}" url="/upload">
                    <upload:attributes>
                        <ObjectAttribute id="_IDGenObjectAttribute1" title="Uploaded by" text="{user}" active="false"/>
                    </upload:attributes>
                </upload:UploadSetItem>
            </upload:UploadSet>
        </content>
        <buttons>        
            <Button id="_IDGenButton1" text="Upload" press="handler.onUploadSet" icon="sap-icon://upload-to-cloud" type="Emphasized"/>
            <Button id="cancel" press="handler.onCloseDialog" text="Cancel" icon="sap-icon://cancel"/>
        </buttons>
	</Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

Then the issue gone .

entry.title: Script layout changes not reflected in print preview
entry.content:
Dear Team,

When ever we try to change with SPACES,TEXT FIELDS,BINDINGS in SAP Script, That layout changes have been reflected from development client to testing client through SCC1N.

Then execute the test print preview in SE71 it's working fine but execution with driver program(Z-Tcode) it's not reflected in print preview/print/spool.

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Script layout changes not reflected in print preview
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

If the print preview in SE71 is working fine,it means the data has been transported to test client correctly.

If in your customized program you are not getting correct print preview,pls help confirm how your program calls the sascript form
and if you are using the same output device in Z program as in the test with SE71.
You can also run report RSTXDELL to delete sapscript load and then test again.

Best Regards

SAP Support

entry.title: Build apps backend session not released
entry.content:
Hi

We have conenected Build apps to S4 backend vua BTP, SCC and Gateway OData service

When App is openened in "Preview" mode, it creates 7 dialogue sessions in the backened

Also when the App/browser is closed, backend session is not released in S4

Anyone faced this issue

Regards

MB

entry.title: Aussie looking to learn new things
entry.content:
Hello from Australia!

I have recently watched the recording of the International Women's Day online SAP Event and really enjoyed listening to the speakers and the questions which were asked. Well done David, Lin & Sandra!

I have had an SAP Mining career for going close to 17 years now in the Work Management/Accounts Payable/Supply/Technology space (yes wearing the hard hats & steel capped boots) and really do enjoy teaching/coaching others how to use SAP & especially Fiori.

Looking forward to meeting new people & learning new things.

@David_Chaviano @jmmargo

Cypriana S.

entry.title: Re: The Importance of Public Speaking for Visibility of Own Expertise
entry.content:
I believe that it is very important to overcome the fear of being judged and confiding in one's abilities to take the first step towards public speaking. It really helps in exchanging ideas and building meaningful relationships.

It obviously requires a lot of prep and understanding of the audience we are presenting to and always imporves over time. The idea of presenting to a vast audience still gives me burtterflies inmy stomach but I have learnt to still pursue public speaking despite all odds 🙂

entry.title: Failed to load resource: net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR when open SAPGUI app from Fiori
entry.content:
Good day everyone, we are experiencing issue with SAPGUI in Fiori. Every time we open SAPGUI app from Fiori, it always shows blank and when we checked the error in the console, it always shows Failed to load resources: nett::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR 200. Other Fiori apps are fine, it's the transaction/SAPGUI apps that are affected. 

Below, we tried to open TCode 'BP' from Fiori:

TCODE BP from Fiorierrors in console taberrors in network tab

The tile:

target application fieldstiles

What troubleshooting steps can I take to identify the root cause of this issue? Thank you for your time and attention.

Regards.

Our system: 

  • Fiori Embedded
  • DEV client
  • SAP S/4HANA 2022 SP03 (on premise)
  • SAP FIORI FES 2022 FOR S/4HANA SP03
  • ABAP PLATFORM 2022 SP03
  • SAP S/4HANA FOUNDATION 2022 SP03
  • SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2022 SP03
  • SAPUI5 1.108.22 (202308300810)

entry.title: Re: Unique Constraint Violated Error for Master Data
entry.content:
@indu_sharma 

This happens when you have non unique property value being loaded for an attribute. Just check if there are records where you have more than one attribute value against a property for same master data ID.

 

Br.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: EML to create Sales Order not working
entry.content: After executing the EML in a separate class, I was indeed able to see that a mandatory field is missing during commit. Since, inside the behavior implementation class, commit happens internally, there was no way we could find this error. Thanks anyway for your support.

I have to same problem. I checked your issue but it is not solve. Do you have any other advice.

entry.title: Issue in Creating GSTR2 Report ,Unable To Find Assessable Value Data Source and add Storage Location
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Hope you all are doing great,

I am creating GSTR2 Report for Accounts Payable Process in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud. It is a Compliance Report for India. Standard Report is not available so we are creating it by using CDS View app. I am facing Following Errors and unable to proceed further.

  1. I have added Supplier Invoice Data source, while adding Storage Location from Material Document Item data source multiple entries are generating as there is no Data Source available for Line item like Supplier Invoice Item Data Source. Please advise Is there any way to add Storage location so that Multiple entries should not generate.
  2. My client is using Custom Duty Process for which they are booking Custom Duty and GST on Assessable Value but in CDS View, I am unable to get the Assessable Value. Can you Please Suggest if any Data Source is available for Assessable value or How to add the Tax amount calculated on Assessable value. 

 

 

Thanks and Regards,

Shreshtha Pareek

And my question is : Do I have to run the tcode FINS_UPD_CMP_VERSN on each SAP client ?

Cypriana S.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics cloud - Product - Material Master data
entry.content:
hi,

my suggestion

use -user exits = template load via tcode mm91

or 

mm tcode=mass

regards,

vijay kalbhor

entry.title: Oracle linux/OS upgrade
entry.content:
Hello team,

ALL our sap systems are on AWS with oracle database and oracle linux 7.x. since support is getting expired this year end, we would like to have some recommendations from you; 

  1. what is the best/recommended approach for upgrade --> in-place or out-of-place/fresh install
  2. Any SAP notes/documentation referring to oracle linux upgrade?
  3. Do you recommend any technical procedure for the same. 

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Oracle linux/OS upgrade
entry.content:
In regards to your request for recommendations, it generally depends on your company's needs, available resources, downtime allowance and expertise.

  1. In-Place vs Out-of-Place Upgrade: These are the two main approaches available for your situation. An in-place upgrade involves updating the current system that your SAP systems are running on, while an out-of-place upgrade indicates installing a new version of the system and migrating your SAP applications to that version.

    • In-Place: This would result in less work overall since you are not reinstalling everything from scratch, but it could come with risks as things might not go as planned which may impact your system uptime.

    • Out-of-Place: This approach would be somehow safer as you can parallelly run the new system while keeping the old one operational, providing you with a better rollback plan if issues arise. However, it requires more resources & effort as it involves setting up new systems and migrating existing applications.

  2. please refer to note 2069760 - Oracle Linux 7.x SAP Installation and Upgrade

  3. ghly recommended to involve your IT together with Oracle support during the planning and execution of the upgrade. Ensure that you have a proper backup and recovery strategy in place as well as thorough testing procedures to verify that all your SAP applications function as expected after the upgrade. You should also plan for necessary employee training for any functional changes from OS upgrade.

Remember, ultimately the final decision depends on your specific circumstance and should be done based on thorough planning, risk analysis and testing.

entry.title: MIGO ERROR: The system could not create any outputs
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

If I click the individual slip check box, Then it gives the message "The system could not create any outputs". output will not trigger. I have checked in debugging mode, always xnast is empty, so it gives the message.

 

Thanks with regards,

Vallamuthu M.

entry.title: 'INTO' is invalid (due to grammar) error while SQL in BAPI
entry.content:
Hi guys!

The error occurs in the first INTO of the first SELECT. I have 'INTO' is invalid (due to grammar)
I need to return several tables that will become arrays in an application that will call this BAPI remotely (RFC). However, when I add this code to the SAP that will run the RFC, it gives a problem with the INTO clause.
INTO is invalid here (due to grammar), but I don't know what I'm doing wrong, because I've already run this function in other SAPs and it worked normally.
The logic of this RFC is to return the data from the MARD, MARC, MAKT and MBEW tables based on the MARA table. Then I perform a select with where in the MARA table that will return a series of items, and based on the code of these items I perform a FOR ALL ENTRIES to search for these same materials in other tables and return them separately. I can't use joins. And then I come across the INTO clause error when I use anything between the word MARA (FROM MARA) and INTO. (In this case it's the order by)
I need the up to to be able to limit the lines of this return.
Can anyone help me?

Code (also attached): 

FUNCTION Z_GET_MATERIAL_FULL_CUSTOM.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*" IMPORTING
*" REFERENCE(MARA_MAX_ROWS) TYPE INT4
*" REFERENCE(MARA_SKIP_ROWS) TYPE INT4
*" TABLES
*" MARA_TABLE STRUCTURE MARA
*" MARD_TABLE STRUCTURE MARD
*" MARC_TABLE STRUCTURE MARC
*" MAKT_TABLE STRUCTURE MAKT
*" MBEW_TABLE STRUCTURE MBEW
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
SELECT MATNR, MEINS, MTPOS_MARA FROM MARA WHERE MTPOS_MARA = 'ZMAP' OR MTPOS_MARA = 'ZMAN' ORDER BY MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @Mara_TABLE UP TO @Mara_MAX_ROWS ROWS OFFSET @Mara_SKIP_ROWS .

SELECT MATNR, LABST, LGPBE FROM MARD FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @MARD_TABLE.


SELECT MATNR, MINBE, MABST, PLIFZ, DISMM, STEUC FROM MARC FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @Marc_TABLE.


SELECT MATNR, MAKTX FROM MAKT FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR AND SPRAS = 'P' INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @makt_TABLE.


SELECT MATNR, VERPR FROM MBEW FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @Mara_TABLE WHERE MATNR = @Mara_TABLE-MATNR INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @MBEW_TABLE.


ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Synching Folder Structure between SEN Producer and SEN Manager
entry.content:
Hi All,

In SEN Producer, I created a folder structure as shown in the picture below. I created the Content folder structure outside Unsorted. 

SEN Producer Screen with Folder Structure

Next, when I opened the SEN Manager and done the refresh, the Content folder structure created in SEN Producer successfully appears in SEN Manager, but it appears under Unsorted by default, as shown in the picture below.

SEN Manager Screen

My Question: When the SEN Manager is synched with SEN Producer, should the SEN manager show/fetch the folder structure similar to the SEN Producer? In my case, in SEN Producer, the Content folder appears outside Unsorted, but my SEN Manager shows the Content folder inside Unsorted when synched.

entry.title: Re: Handling of bank charges in bank statement
entry.content: Hi Bohdan, many thanks for preparing this. I've tried to replicate this in our system but the posting area 2 still doesn't post the bank charges but the debit and credit was already deducted the bank charges are maintained in FEBEP-SPESK. I found in other blogs that Posting type for the posting rule of posting area 2 must be 7 or 8 , clearing the subledger. Is there any other possible way? Thanks in advance.

Thank you!

Phaneendra

entry.title: SAP Build IRPA not recognize screen on virtual machine
entry.content:
Hello, 

we have a virtual machine where we have out unattended agent. I have created an automation on my personal computer and it is working fine there. Unfortunately the desktop agent at the virtual machine is not recognizing the second screen of the automation. When I loaded traces, it looks like that the agent is not even aware of that the screen is loaded (see screenshots, there is no information about screen loaded but when I start it on my PC, the screen is loaded). I can see that the screen is loaded at the virtual machine, but the agent did not cach it. Do you have any ideas why is this happening?

Regards,

Preeti

entry.title: Re: Migration cockpit in fiori
entry.content:
Yes, in Migration Cockpit you can simulate the migration process before running the data upload so that you can detect the errors in advance. Please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/da6a3a95ee7d44ea8845bbfc3889a318.html?q=migration%20cockpit&locale=en-US

Thanks.

In MRS Planning Board when we try to create automatic assignment we are getting below error:

Auto. assgmnt creation for demand 0000XXXXXXXXX / 0010 failed; partner is not available

Can you please us create automatic assignment.

Regards,

Preeti

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the launch of the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal - your one-stop destination for all learning offerings related to SAP Cloud ALM. With a comprehensive range of learning materials, structured learning paths, and additional resources, this platform provides an exceptional learning experience to help individuals succeed in their roles. Let's explore what this exciting new platform has to offer.

Featured Learning Offering: Strategic Topics with SAP Cloud ALM

The SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal kicks off with its latest featured learning offerings, regularly updated to provide the most popular and relevant opportunities. Currently, there are two featured courses:

  • Solution Transformation Consultant Training

This course is designed for consultants responsible for driving business transformation using an end-to-end tool chain. It covers the design-to-deploy phase, including SAP Cloud ALM and Tricentis Test Automation for SAP.

  • SAP Cloud ALM Implementation and Operations Configuration Webinar Series

This webinar series helps users efficiently manage projects, optimize processes, orchestrate tests, and gain insights through analytics. Recorded sessions are available for replay.

SAP Cloud ALM Learning Journeys: Learn at Your Own Pace

Our learning journeys on learning.sap.com offer a structured pathway to master SAP Cloud ALM at your preferred pace. These journeys are designed to cater to diverse learning preferences while ensuring flexibility and convenience. Whether you're new to SAP Cloud ALM or seeking to enhance your existing skills, our personalized learning paths are tailored to meet your needs and goals.

Additional Learning Resources: Expanding Your Knowledge

This section provides additional opportunities to deepen your understanding of SAP Cloud ALM. It includes:

  • Learning Offerings for Supported Solutions

If you have a specific solution focus, check out this part which directs you to the dedicated content for your SAP products. You'll also find information on how SAP Cloud ALM supports the Activate Methodology and CLEAN CORE.

  • How-to and deep-dive learning materials

For those seeking hands-on learning as an expert, this section collects expert-level knowledge of SAP Cloud ALM.

  • Continuous Learning Opportunities: Events and Beyond

Stay connected with like-minded professionals, thought leaders, and industry experts by participating in ALM events and other channels that provide valuable content and foster community interactions.

Regardless of whether you are a customer or a partner, the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal caters to your specific learning needs. It is designed to accommodate individuals at all skill levels, from beginners to advanced users, and offers continuous learning opportunities to help users stay up to date with the latest advancements in SAP Cloud ALM.

Don't miss out on the opportunity to enhance your understanding of SAP Cloud ALM. Take advantage of the SAP Cloud ALM Learning Portal today and discover how it can elevate your knowledge and expertise to new heights. Start your learning journey now and unlock the full potential of SAP Cloud ALM.

entry.title: Re: SAP Basis Learning: Work processes
entry.content:
Hello,

If you like this video you can check also one about starting SAP with sapcontrol. It includes an explanation of how the mechanism works with examples and diagrams. Beginners may also find the startup troubleshooting session interesting. Enjoy.
